failed_checks.check_name: Ensure the S3 bucket has access logging enabled
failed_checks.description: S3 bucket 'payment-receipts-prod' does not have server access logging enabled. Audit trail missing for compliance.
failed_checks.remediation: Add logging block: logging { target_bucket = aws_s3_bucket.log_bucket.id target_prefix = 'payment-receipts/' }

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure the S3 bucket does not allow public read access
failed_checks.description: S3 bucket ACL set to public-read. Payment receipt documents exposed to internet.
failed_checks.remediation: Change acl to 'private'. Enable S3 Block Public Access via aws_s3_bucket_public_access_block resource.

failed_checks.description: ALB listener accepts unencrypted HTTP traffic on port 80 without redirecting to HTTPS.
failed_checks.remediation: Change listener action to redirect to HTTPS: default_action { type = 'redirect'; redirect { protocol = 'HTTPS'; status_code = 'HTTP_301' } }

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure KMS CMK rotation is enabled
failed_checks.description: KMS key used for RDS encryption does not have automatic rotation enabled.
failed_checks.remediation: Set enable_key_rotation = true. Key rotation occurs annually and is transparent to applications.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure RDS database has encryption at rest enabled
failed_checks.description: RDS instance 'analytics-db' storage encryption disabled. PII data at rest unencrypted.
failed_checks.remediation: Set storage_encrypted = true and specify kms_key_id. Note: requires recreating the instance.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure RDS is not publicly accessible
failed_checks.description: Production RDS instance is publicly accessible. PostgreSQL port 5432 exposed to internet.
failed_checks.remediation: Set publicly_accessible = false. Ensure RDS is in private subnet with no internet gateway route.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 22
failed_checks.description: Security group allows unrestricted inbound SSH from the entire internet.
failed_checks.remediation: Replace 0.0.0.0/0 with specific corporate IP CIDR. Consider AWS Systems Manager Session Manager as a no-port-22 alternative.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure no security groups allow ingress from 0.0.0.0/0 to port 3389
failed_checks.description: RDP (port 3389) open to internet. High risk of brute force and ransomware delivery.
failed_checks.remediation: Restrict to trusted IPs only. Use VPN or AWS Systems Manager for remote access.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure DynamoDB Tables are encrypted using KMS
failed_checks.description: DynamoDB table 'session-store' not configured with customer-managed KMS key encryption.
failed_checks.remediation: Add: server_side_encryption { enabled = true; kms_key_arn = aws_kms_key.dynamo_key.arn }

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure S3 buckets should have event notifications enabled
failed_checks.description: S3 bucket has no event notifications configured. Object access and changes not tracked.
failed_checks.remediation: Configure aws_s3_bucket_notification to send events to SNS/SQS/Lambda for audit logging.

failed_checks.code_block: # securityContext not defined — defaults to root
failed_checks.description: Container 'payment-api' runs as root (UID 0). Container escape gives attacker root on the host.
failed_checks.remediation: Add securityContext: runAsNonRoot: true; runAsUser: 1000; readOnlyRootFilesystem: true

failed_checks.description: No livenessProbe configured. Unhealthy containers will not be restarted automatically.
failed_checks.remediation: Add livenessProbe with httpGet or exec check and appropriate failureThreshold.

failed_checks.check_name: Image Tag should be fixed — not latest or blank
failed_checks.description: Container using ':latest' image tag. Non-deterministic deployments. Supply chain risk.
failed_checks.remediation: Pin to a specific SHA or version tag: envoyproxy/envoy:v1.29.2@sha256:...

failed_checks.check_name: Minimize the admission of containers with added capability NET_RAW
failed_checks.description: Container has NET_RAW and SYS_ADMIN capabilities added. Enables low-level network attacks and host manipulation.
failed_checks.remediation: Drop all capabilities and add only the minimum required. Drop ALL first: capabilities: { drop: [ALL]; add: [NET_BIND_SERVICE] }

failed_checks.check_name: Containers should not run with allowPrivilegeEscalation
failed_checks.description: Init container allows privilege escalation. Process can gain more privileges than its parent.
failed_checks.remediation: Set allowPrivilegeEscalation: false on all containers and init containers.

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure that HEALTHCHECK instructions have been added to the container image
failed_checks.description: No HEALTHCHECK instruction in Dockerfile. Container orchestrator cannot determine container health.
failed_checks.remediation: Add: HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=5s --retries=3 CMD curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure that a user for the container has been created
failed_checks.description: Dockerfile has no USER instruction. Container runs as root by default.
failed_checks.remediation:
Add: RUN addgroup --gid 1001 appgroup && adduser --uid 1001 --gid 1001 appuser
USER 1001

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure the base image uses a non-latest version tag
failed_checks.description: Dockerfile uses 'python:latest' base image. Non-reproducible builds. Unknown base image CVEs.
failed_checks.remediation: Pin to a specific version with digest: FROM python:3.12.2-slim@sha256:...

failed_checks.check_name: Ensure that COPY is used instead of ADD in Dockerfiles
failed_checks.description: ADD instruction used instead of COPY. ADD can auto-extract archives and fetch URLs — unexpected behavior possible.
failed_checks.remediation: Replace ADD with COPY for local files: COPY ./config /app/config
